questions.question: Explain the role of version control systems in Agile software development and mention two popular tools.
keyPoints: Version control tracks code changes and enables team collaboration
Supports branching and merging for parallel development
Maintains code history and facilitates code review
Popular tools: Git, GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket

questions.question: What is CI/CD? List three benefits of implementing CI/CD in Agile projects.
keyPoints: CI/CD stands for Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery/Deployment
Benefits: Faster release cycles, Early bug detection, Improved code quality
Automated testing ensures reliability
Enables frequent and reliable deployments
Reduces manual effort and human errors

questions.question: Describe the purpose of a burndown chart in Agile and explain how it helps teams.
keyPoints: Burndown chart shows remaining work vs time during a sprint
Helps track progress toward sprint goal
Identifies if team is ahead or behind schedule
Enables early detection of blockers and risks
Aids in sprint forecasting and planning

questions.question: List five categories of tools required for a comprehensive Agile tool set.
keyPoints: Project Management tools (Jira, Trello)
Version Control (Git, GitHub)
CI/CD tools (Jenkins, Travis CI)
Testing tools (Selenium, JUnit)
Collaboration tools (Slack, Teams)
Communication and documentation tools

questions.question: Explain the concept of Kanban board and its advantages in Agile project management.
keyPoints: Kanban board is a visual tool for managing work flow
Uses columns: To Do, In Progress, Done
Limits Work In Progress (WIP) to improve focus
Provides real-time visibility of project status
Reduces bottlenecks and improves delivery speed

questions.question: What is Docker and how does it support Agile development practices?
keyPoints: Docker is a containerization platform
Packages applications with dependencies
Ensures consistency across environments
Facilitates faster deployment and scaling
Supports microservices architecture common in Agile

questions.question: Discuss the importance of automated testing tools in Agile methodology.
keyPoints: Enables continuous testing in CI/CD pipelines
Provides faster feedback on code changes
Supports regression testing for frequent changes
Improves code quality and reduces defects
Tools include Selenium, JUnit, TestNG, pytest

questions.question: Describe the Agile tool ecosystem. How do these tools work together to support continuous delivery? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: Project management tools manage backlogs and sprints
Version control stores and versions source code
CI/CD tools automate build, test, and deployment
Testing tools ensure quality through automation
Collaboration tools enable team communication
Integration between tools creates automated workflows
Example: GitHub code change triggers Jenkins pipeline which runs tests and deploys to container

questions.question: Explain the different Agile ceremonies and how tool support enhances each of them.
keyPoints: Sprint Planning: Tools like Jira help plan sprint scope and assign story points
Daily Standup: Tools show task status and identify blockers
Sprint Review: Demo tools showcase completed features to stakeholders
Sprint Retrospective: Tools like FunRetro collect feedback and action items
Backlog Grooming: Tools help prioritize and refine user stories
Visual boards provide transparency for all ceremonies

questions.question: Compare Jira and Trello as Agile project management tools. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Jira: Enterprise-level, complex workflows, extensive reporting, suitable for large teams
Trello: Simple, visual Kanban boards, suitable for small teams and simple projects
Jira supports Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid methodologies
Trello uses cards and boards with drag-and-drop interface
Choose Jira for: Large projects, complex workflows, compliance needs
Choose Trello for: Simplicity, quick onboarding, visual task management

questions.question: Explain the concept of DevOps tools in Agile. How do they bridge the gap between development and operations?
keyPoints: DevOps tools support continuous delivery and infrastructure as code
Version control for infrastructure (Terraform, Ansible)
Container orchestration (Kubernetes) for deployment management
Monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana) for production insights
Automation reduces handoff delays between teams
Tools like Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes create complete delivery pipeline
Enables faster feedback from production to development
